gallery.text.1: 3/8" bolt cut as blank for clamp screw.
gallery.text.2: Screw with two chamfers cut. The smaller one was peined over a smaller washer to allow the large washer to move freely, though it was effectively a captive washer.
gallery.text.4: Jig to clamp mudflap washer so that it could be domed.
gallery.text.5: One finished screw clamp with captive washer to form a load-dispersing surface.
gallery.text.10: Acid bath to clean scale of steel strip.
gallery.text.11: 50mm x 5mm steel strip that will form the body of the clamp, marked out, and pilot holes drilled.
gallery.text.12: Clamp body drilled and tapped. Clamp-screws in place, and 1/4" retainer screws, for controller, in place.
gallery.text.16: Clamp body has been formed. The intent had been to score and cold-forge the workpiece but the attempt to form the second fold results in mechanicalfailure of the first fold. The workpiece was welded instead.
gallery.text.17: A view of the underside of the clamp-screws. The peined ends and retainer washers can be seen recessed into the larger, domed washers.
gallery.text.18: Rubber was cut and clued to the screws, and the underside of the clamp that contacts the desk surface. This had a two-fold purpose: To protext teh desk surfaces, and to reduce slipping. A block of wood was clamped in place to hold the rubber whilst the glue cured. Two knobs were formed from Kirks softdrink bottle caps filled with 'Kneadit' and jammed onto the keyed screw shanks.
